trending_themes.theme: Historic Grammy win — first K-pop producer
trending_themes.evidence: Won Grammy Award for production on 'Golden'; also won Academy Award and Golden Globe for Best Original Song. First K-pop producer to achieve Grammy.

trending_themes.theme: The Black Label Series B — 1 trillion won valuation
trending_themes.evidence: Completed 120B won ($79.8M) Series B funding May 20, 2026. Post-valuation: 1 trillion won. Investors: Tencent Music Entertainment, Krafton.
229 production credits, 688 tracks written. Produced for YG artists: Big Bang, 2NE1, BLACKPINK, Lee Hi, Somi, Sunmi.

annotation_hooks: Teddy Park's Grammy win represents K-pop production crossing into Western awards infrastructure — validates a production style built on K-pop's melodic hooks, rap breaks, and empowerment framing that he helped define with 2NE1 and BLACKPINK
The Black Label's 1 trillion won valuation signals institutional validation of K-pop production excellence beyond idol marketing
BLACKPINK songs annotated as 'produced by Teddy' carry extra weight: DDU-DU, Kill This Love, Pink Venom are all his sonic signature
raw_headlines: Teddy Park Wins First K-pop Grammy for Golden
The Black Label Completes Series B at 1T Won Valuation
Academy Award and Golden Globe for Best Original Song
The Black Label Series B — Tencent and Krafton as Investors
